vcenter.name: vCenter
steps: Upgrade vCenter to version 9.1 using standard vSphere upgrade process - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: Integrated Windows Authentication on vCenter
bullets: Replace Integrated Windows Authentication configured with another IDP configuration

esx.name: ESX
steps: All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: ESX bootbank
bullets: Validate ESX bootbank is at least 1gb in size by running command on host df -h /bootbank

notices.title: Check HCL for Hardware support on VCF 9.1
bullets: Intel Skylake (Broadwell, Kabylake & Skylake) Processors no longer supported (deprecated in earlier releases), requires RPQ

operations.name: VCF Operations Upgrade
steps: Upgrade VCF Operations to version 9.1 compatible release (standalone) - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: VCF Operations Information
bullets: Decommissioning of the 9.0 Fleet Management Appliance: VCF 9.1 completely replaces the standalone Fleet Management Appliance introduced in version 9.0. It is replaced with two new services—Fleet Lifecycle and SDDC Lifecycle—which run natively within VCF management services. The Fleet Management appliance is powered down and ready to be decommissioned.

sub_bullets: When attempting to upgrade to VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF) 9.1, users may find that while the version 9.1 option is available in the SDDC Manager Binary Management dropdown menu, no binaries are displayed for any category (Upgrade, Patch, or Install) - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
Upgrading VCF Operations from 9.0.2 to 9.1 returns sync error when upgrading ESXi host - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

license.name: License Server

tables.title: Required Network Configuration
tables.description: Prepare the following IP addresses and DNS entries before deployment:

steps: Deploy License Server - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

sub_bullets: License assignment fails upgrading vCenter from 9.0.x to 9.1. - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).
Licenses could not be assigned to vCenters during license assignment on VCF 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

logs.name: Log Management
steps: Deploy new Log Management 9.1 services - there is no in-place upgrade path. All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

network.name: VCF Operations for Network
steps: Upgrade VCF Operations for Network to version 9.1 compatible - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

sub_bullets: Upgrading VCF 9.0 environment to 9.1 fails when trying to Import VCF Operations for Networks in Fleet Lifecycle - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

vidb.name: Identity Broker
steps: Identity Broker Upgrade - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

replication.name: vSphere Replication
steps: Upgrade vSphere Replication to version 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

srm.name: Site Recovery Manager
steps: Upgrade Site Recovery Manager to version 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

avi.name: AVI (NSX ALB)
steps: Upgrade NSX Advanced Load Balancer to compatible version - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

hcx.name: VMware HCX
steps: Upgrade HCX to version compatible with vSphere 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

supervisor.name: VMware vSphere Supervisor
steps: Upgrade VMware vSphere Supervisor to version 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

vsan_witness.name: vSAN Witness Host
steps: Upgrade vSAN Witness host to version 9.1 - will happen as part of ESX upgrade workflow in VCF Operations

sddc_manager.name: SDDC Manager
steps: Upgrade SDDC Manager to 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

vcfms.name: VCF Management Service Deployment
steps: Deploy VCF Mnaagement Services for 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: VCF Management Services
bullets: Minimum 12 IP addresses required for deployment.

bullets.text: An additional 18 IP addresses can be added afterwards for the following scenarios:
sub_bullets: New VCF management services are added.
Scale out of VCF Management services or new nodes as upgrade to future versions take place.
A maximum of 30 IP address is required, these can be added as new IP ranges in the Lifecycle section of the VCF Operations UI, these ranges do not need to be continuous.
All IP Ranges must be on the VCF Management Network.

An internal IP range is used by VCF services runtime, this IP range is 198.18.0.0/15, it is important to ensure this IP range does not overlap with the IP range of your management network, this internal network can be changed to 240.0.0.0/15 or 250.0.0.0/15 by using JSON specification file to deploy.

automation.name: VCF Automation Upgrade
steps: Upgrade VCF Automation to 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: VCF Automation Components
bullets: The FQDN from the existing Automation instance is automatically transferred as VCF Automation FQDN of the 9.1 instance.
If your existing Automation instance uses an external load balancer, no changes in its configuration are required.
VCF Automation 9.0.x IP addresses of the existing nodes are freed after the upgrade.

notices.title: Resource Requirements
bullets: Ensure sufficient resources are available for all Automation components
Review minimum requirements in documentation

nsx.name: NSX
steps: Upgrade NSX Local Managers to 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: NSX Version Compatibility
bullets: Ensure NSX version is compatible with vSphere 9.1
Review VMware Product Interoperability Matrix

nsx_federation.name: NSX Federation
steps: Upgrade NSX Global Managers to 9.1 - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: Federation Prerequisites
bullets: All NSX sites must be on compatible versions
Network connectivity required between all sites
Upgrade BEFORE NSX Local Managers

vxrail.name: Dell VxRail
steps: Please Reach out to Dell on Steps and Services

notices.title: VxRail Upgrades
bullets: All upgrades for VxRail Appliances will be validated through Dell support team or Dell Field/Partner(s).There are different pre-check and Solve Desktop Procedure based on the VxRail Hardware and VxRail versions. It is recommended not to attempt online upgrade through VxRail Manager UI/VxL Manager vCenter Plugin.

vidb_overlay_transition.name: Transition Identity Broker to VCF Management Network
steps: Transition Identity Broker 9.0.x Instance to the VCF Management Network - All Guidance is provided in link (View Documentation).

notices.title: This step must be completed first
bullets: If Identity Broker 9.0.x is on a non-mgmt network (regular vlan or NSX overlay) – please contact support for guidance and there will be downtime during the upgrade for VMware Identity Broker and Single Sign-On.
